title: Coordinator re-run loop (rounds, idempotent re-spawn, early-stop)
description: When a blackboard is configured, run the children for up to maxRounds rounds sharing the blackboard, scaffolding each child's config ONLY on round 1 and re-spawning `agent-bober run` each round, and EARLY-STOPPING when a completed round adds zero new findings; when no blackboard is configured, run exactly one pass byte-identically to today. Add a blackboard-aware path to FleetCoordinator (e.g. executeWithBlackboard(manifest, { dbPath, namespace, maxRounds }) OR extend execute to branch on manifest.blackboard): for round r in 1..maxRounds — run mapBounded(children, concurrency, child => runChildRound(rootDir, child, r)) where runChildRound scaffolds (writes config incl. the fleet section from Sprint 2) ONLY when r===1 and the child folder is not already scaffolded, then spawns `agent-bober run <task>` in the child cwd; after each round, count active 'finding' facts via the shared blackboard and break when the count did not increase. Wire into runFleet: compute resolveBlackboardPath once, ensureDir the db dir, open the SharedBlackboard, run the rounds (or the single pass), close the blackboard, and keep writing the existing fleet-report.json from the FINAL round's outcomes. Per-child failures stay data (never throw); only batch-setup errors throw (existing exit-0 contract preserved).

successCriteria.description: Build + typecheck pass with zero errors.
Full suite passes; only the 6 known cockpit-integration MCP failures may fail; existing fleet coordinator/runner/index tests pass unchanged.

With a blackboard configured (maxRounds=N), the coordinator runs the children up to N rounds; round 1 scaffolds each child config, rounds 2..N re-spawn `agent-bober run` WITHOUT re-scaffolding/clobbering the round-1 config. A test with an injected fake runner/scaffolder asserts scaffold is called once per child and the runner is called per round.

Early-stop: when a completed round adds zero new 'finding' facts to the blackboard, the loop stops before maxRounds. A test seeds findings so round 2 adds none and asserts the loop ran exactly 2 rounds (not N).

When NO blackboard is configured, the coordinator runs exactly one pass (round 1) and its behavior + return value are byte-identical to the pre-Phase-B coordinator.execute — the existing coordinator/runner tests pass unchanged and a test asserts the single-pass path opens no blackboard.

runFleet computes resolveBlackboardPath once, ensures the db directory exists, opens/closes the SharedBlackboard around the rounds, and threads the absolute path into each child's scaffolded config; a test asserts the path is injected (child config has the fleet section) and the rounds execute.

Per-child failures remain data: a child whose spawn fails does not abort the round or the run; the loop completes and the report still builds (exit-0 contract). A test with a failing fake child asserts no throw and a populated report.
lint passes; the rounds loop is additive and gated on manifest.blackboard; the never-throw fleet contract and the no-blackboard byte-identical path are preserved.

nonGoals: Do NOT write fleet-synthesis.json yet (Sprint 4) — this sprint runs the rounds + keeps the existing fleet-report.json.
Do NOT auto-publish findings on the children's behalf — findings come only from task prompts calling `agent-bober blackboard` (Sprint 2).
Do NOT add semantic convergence judging — early-stop is purely 'no new findings this round'.
Do NOT change the no-blackboard single-pass behavior (must stay byte-identical).
Do NOT re-scaffold/clobber a child's round-1 config on later rounds.
stopConditions: The coordinator runs up to maxRounds rounds (scaffold once, re-spawn each round), early-stops on no-new-findings, stays byte-identical single-pass without a blackboard, preserves the exit-0 contract, and build/typecheck/test/lint are green
definitionOfDone: FleetCoordinator runs a bounded rounds loop when a blackboard is configured (scaffold round 1 only, re-spawn each round, early-stop on no-new-findings) and a byte-identical single pass otherwise; runFleet computes+injects the absolute blackboard path, opens/closes the SharedBlackboard around the rounds, and still writes fleet-report.json from the final round. Collocated tests cover: scaffold-once/re-spawn-per-round, early-stop, byte-identical single pass, path injection, and the failing-child exit-0 path. Build/typecheck/test/lint green.
assumptions: FleetCoordinator.execute(manifest) (coordinator.ts:29) currently does mapBounded(children, concurrency, child => runChild(rootDir, child)); runChild scaffolds then spawns. The rounds loop wraps this: scaffold gating on round 1 + folder-exists, re-spawn each round.

Scaffolder.scaffold (Sprint 2) writes the fleet section when given the resolved blackboard config; on rounds 2..N the child folder already has bober.config.json so scaffolding is skipped (idempotent) — confirm the scaffolder already no-ops or guard it in the loop.

runFleet (index.ts:113-124) calls coordinator.execute then aggregates+reports; the rounds loop replaces the single execute when manifest.blackboard is set, returning the final round's executions for the existing aggregate+report path.
'New findings' is measured by the active 'finding' count in the namespace before vs after a round via the SharedBlackboard (readAll().length).
The DI seams in src/fleet/coordinator.test.ts and index.test.ts (fake scaffolder/runner/coordinator) are reused to test the loop without real child processes.
generatorNotes:
Read FIRST: src/fleet/coordinator.ts in FULL (execute :29, runChild :39 — the never-reject thunk; scaffolder+runner DI in the constructor); src/fleet/coordinator.test.ts (the fake scaffolder/runner pattern); src/fleet/index.ts (runFleet — coordinator.execute at :118, aggregate :120, report build+write :124); src/fleet/runner.ts (ChildRunner.run — execa agent-bober run); src/fleet/scaffolder.ts (Sprint 2 — writes the fleet section; confirm it no-ops or can be guarded when the folder exists); src/fleet/shared-blackboard.ts (Sprint 1 — readAll for the new-findings count); arch ADR-3/ADR-5 + the CP4 data-flow in .bober/architecture/arch-20260618-heterogeneous-multi-provider-agent-team-architecture.md.

Design: add FleetCoordinator.executeRounds(manifest, blackboard, { maxRounds }) OR branch inside execute on manifest.blackboard. Pseudocode for the blackboard path: let prevCount = blackboard.readAll().length; let lastExecutions; for (let r = 1; r <= maxRounds; r++) { lastExecutions = await mapBounded(children, concurrency, child => this.runChildRound(rootDir, child, r, blackboardScaffoldCfg)); const count = blackboard.readAll().length; if (r > 1 && count === prevCount) break; prevCount = count; } return lastExecutions; where runChildRound scaffolds only when r===1 (and uses the Sprint-2 scaffolder threading to write the fleet section), and always spawns runner.run({cwd, task}). No-blackboard path: keep the existing single mapBounded execute EXACTLY (branch: if (!manifest.blackboard) return this.execute(manifest)).

runFleet: const dbPath = resolveBlackboardPath(effectiveManifest); if (dbPath) { await ensureDir(dirname(dbPath)); const bb = SharedBlackboard.open({dbPath, namespace, maxRounds}); try { executions = await coordinator.executeRounds(effectiveManifest, bb, {maxRounds}); } finally { bb.close(); } } else { executions = await coordinator.execute(effectiveManifest); } then the UNCHANGED aggregate + reporter.build + write fleet-report.json.

Tests (coordinator.test.ts + index.test.ts additions): fake scaffolder records calls (assert 1 per child across N rounds); fake runner records (round, folder) per call (assert called each round); a fake blackboard whose readAll() returns a growing then flat list to drive early-stop (assert loop count); no-blackboard path uses the existing execute (assert single mapBounded pass, no blackboard opened); failing fake child → no throw, report built. Use real SharedBlackboard with a tmp db for an integration-ish test if practical. Follow ESM .js imports, import type, unicode section headers.
evaluatorNotes: sc-3-3: inject fakes; run with maxRounds=3 and confirm scaffolder.scaffold called exactly once per child and runner.run called 3 times per child (or until early-stop). sc-3-4: drive the fake/real blackboard so round 2 adds no new findings → assert the loop ran 2 rounds, not 3. sc-3-5 (CRITICAL byte-identical): a no-blackboard manifest → assert coordinator runs ONE mapBounded pass, opens no SharedBlackboard, and the existing coordinator/runner/index tests pass unchanged. sc-3-6: a blackboard manifest → assert resolveBlackboardPath computed, db dir created, SharedBlackboard opened+closed, and each child's scaffolded config carries the fleet section. sc-3-7: a failing fake child → runFleet resolves (no throw), report built with the failure as data. sc-3-2: FULL suite, only 6 known cockpit failures. Build/typecheck/lint clean.
